neighborhood.name: Nob Hill / Northwest District
neighborhood.description: A vibrant, walkable urban neighborhood in Portland's Northwest District, featuring mixed-use zoning along NW 23rd Avenue with independent shops, diverse restaurants, and historic residential streets.

walkability.description: Excellent walkability — one of Portland's most pedestrian-friendly neighborhoods

key_indicators.pedestrian_infrastructure: Extensive sidewalk network with pedestrian-activated crosswalks throughout the district
key_indicators.mixed_use_zoning: Ground-floor retail along NW 23rd Avenue and NW Irving Street provides daily needs within walking distance
key_indicators.transit_proximity: Multiple TriMet bus lines serve NW 23rd Avenue; close to the NW 23rd / NW Thurman transit corridor
key_indicators.bikeability: Buffered bike lanes on NW 23rd Ave; part of Portland's bike boulevard network
key_indicators.street_grid: Dense, interconnected street pattern ideal for walking and cycling

housing.mix: Predominantly single-family homes with some duplexes and small apartment buildings
housing.zoning: Primarily single-family residential (R2, R3 zones); mixed-use along NW 23rd Ave commercial corridor
housing.notable_features: Historic district character, mature street tree canopy, frequent small-lot housing
